design_philosophy.core_loop: Select robot master stage in any order → defeat boss → absorb weapon → use weapon strategically in subsequent stages → progress to linear endgame → defeat final bosses
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Pattern recognition and weapon selection strategy: identifying which robot master weapon is most effective against other robot masters creates a meta-puzzle layer atop platforming execution
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Becoming an adaptive combat robot who grows in power by absorbing defeated enemies' abilities; solving the 'weapon weakness circle' puzzle
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Introduces the charged Mega Buster as core mechanic, deliberately changing the game feel from previous entries. Maintains stage-select freedom while introducing narrative reason for final boss twist (Dr. Cossack revealed as unwilling pawn). Treats weapon acquisition as strategic puzzle, not just power fantasy.

difficulty.lives_system: Traditional lives-based system inherited from NES platformer conventions
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpoints; no mid-stage checkpoints within individual stages
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through stage-select order; player can tackle robot masters in any sequence, enabling strategic approach based on current weapon arsenal. Charged Mega Buster reduces execution difficulty, shifting emphasis toward pattern recognition and weapon selection strategy.

progression.upgrade_system: Robot Master weapon absorption: defeating each of 8 Robot Masters grants signature weapon with limited ammo pool. Support items (Rush modes, Wire Adaptor, Balloon Adaptor, Eddie assistance) found hidden within stages provide utility for navigation and resource recovery. Charged Mega Buster serves as universal upgrade independent of stage completion.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Stage-select screen presents 8 robot master stages available from start. Two final linear stages unlock sequentially after all 8 masters defeated. Wily Castle fortress accessed after defeating Dr. Cossack.
replayability_hooks: 8 different stage-select orders create varied weapon acquisition sequences
Hidden adaptors encourage thorough stage exploration
Eddie random item drops add RNG element to resource management
Multiple weapon effectiveness combinations against bosses incentivize experimentation

world_building_techniques: Stage intro cutscenes introducing Robot Masters and their thematic environments
Plot twist: Dr. Cossack revealed as coerced agent of Dr. Wily through kidnapping of his daughter Kalinka
Proto Man intervention at climax shifts narrative from pure antagonism to manipulation reveal
Character introductions: Eddie (support robot) and new Mega Buster lore establish progression in Dr. Light's capabilities
Narrative justification for persistent threat: Despite 'death' in Mega Man 3, Dr. Wily orchestrates events from shadows

boss_design.philosophy: Each robot master exhibits thematic design tied to environment and signature weapon. Bosses teach players their weapon's properties through combat; defeating them grants that weapon for use against subsequent masters, creating strategic dependency chain. Final tier (Cossack, then Wily) escalates through multi-stage fortress with mini-bosses (Cockroach Twins, Mothraya) before ultimate confrontation.
notable_bosses: Toad Man - Amphibious theme, attacks from water environments
Bright Man - Light-based attacks, Flash Stopper weapon disables enemies temporarily
Pharaoh Man - Egyptian pyramid setting, projectile-focused combat
Ring Man - Ring projectile master, geometric attack patterns
Dust Man - Dust-themed environment with cleanup mechanic integrated into stage design
Skull Man - Skull design, skeletal enemy types in stage
Dive Man - Underwater navigation required, aquatic enemies
Drill Man - Drilling mechanics, subterranean environment
Cockroach Twins - Mid-boss pair in Cossack Citadel, tests accumulated weapon effectiveness
Mothraya - Aerial fortress boss with jetpack mechanics (unused fire exhaust sprites suggest design iteration)
Dr. Cossack - Reveals as unwilling antagonist, climactic reveal boss
Dr. Wily - Final boss, traditional series nemesis returns

faction_indicators: Dr. Cossack's Robot Masters (8 themed constructs) vs. Mega Man's Dr. Light alliance
Proto Man as independent agent, eventually siding with Mega Man and Light
Dr. Wily's shadow manipulation creating false antagonism
danger_signals: Robot Masters seizing control of eight global cities (world domination threat escalation from Mega Man 3)
Kidnapping of Kalinka as coercion mechanism reveals desperation in villain tactics
Wily Castle self-destruction sequence signals existential threat to Mega Man
environmental_storytelling: 8 thematically distinct robot master stages telegraph their abilities and design philosophy
Cossack Citadel's icy fortress aesthetic contrasts with previous Wily Castle designs
Progression from robot master stages → Cossack fortress → Wily Castle represents escalating stakes spatially
Stage environments (water, pyramids, dust, skulls) reinforce robot master identities through level design
